children.heading: Tax credit employee stock ownership plan defined
children.label: a Tax credit employee stock ownership plan defined

children.text: meets the requirements of section 401(a),
is designed to invest primarily in employer securities, and
meets the requirements of subsections (b), (c), (d), (e), (f), (g), (h), and () of this section.o

children.heading: Required allocation of employer securities
children.label: b Required allocation of employer securities
A
children.text: the plan provides for the allocation for the plan year of all employer securities transferred to it or purchased by it (because of the requirements of section 41(c)(1)(B)) to the accounts of all participants who are entitled to share in such allocation, and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: for the plan year the allocation to each participant so entitled is an amount which bears substantially the same proportion to the amount of all such securities allocated to all such participants in the plan for that year as the amount of compensation paid to such participant during that year bears to the compensation paid to all such participants during that year.

children.heading: Compensation in excess of $100,000 disregarded
children.label: 2 Compensation in excess of $100,000 disregarded
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), compensation of any participant in excess of the first $100,000 per year shall be disregarded.

children.heading: Determination of compensation
children.label: 3 Determination of compensation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the amount of compensation paid to a participant for any period is the amount of such participant’s compensation (within the meaning of section 415(c)(3)) for such period.

children.heading: Suspension of allocation in certain cases
children.label: 4 Suspension of allocation in certain cases
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), the allocation to the account of any participant which is attributable to the basic employee plan credit or the credit allowed under section 41 (relating to the employee stock ownership credit) may be extended over whatever period may be necessary to comply with the requirements of section 415.1

children.heading: Participants must have nonforfeitable rights
children.label: c Participants must have nonforfeitable rights
children.text: A plan meets the requirements of this subsection only if it provides that each participant has a nonforfeitable right to any employer security allocated to his account.

children.heading: Employer securities must stay in the plan
children.label: d Employer securities must stay in the plan

children.text: death, disability, separation from service, or termination of the plan;

a transfer of a participant to the employment of an acquiring employer from the employment of the selling corporation in the case of a sale to the acquiring corporation of substantially all of the assets used by the selling corporation in a trade or business conducted by the selling corporation, or
with respect to the stock of a selling corporation, a disposition of such selling corporation’s interest in a subsidiary when the participant continues employment with such subsidiary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A plan meets the requirements of this subsection if it meets the requirements of paragraph (2) or (3), whichever is applicable.

children.heading: Requirements where employer has a registration-type class of securities
children.label: 2 Requirements where employer has a registration-type class of securities
children.text: If the employer has a registration-type class of securities, the plan meets the requirements of this paragraph only if each participant or beneficiary in the plan is entitled to direct the plan as to the manner in which securities of the employer which are entitled to vote and are allocated to the account of such participant or beneficiary are to be voted.

children.heading: Requirement for other employers
children.label: 3 Requirement for other employers
children.text: If the employer does not have a registration-type class of securities, the plan meets the requirements of this paragraph only if each participant or beneficiary in the plan is entitled to direct the plan as to the manner in which voting rights under securities of the employer which are allocated to the account of such participant or beneficiary are to be exercised with respect to any corporate matter which involves the voting of such shares with respect to the approval or disapproval of any corporate merger or consolidation, recapitalization, reclassification, liquidation, dissolution, sale of substantially all assets of a trade or business, or such similar transaction as the Secretary may prescribe in regulations.

children.label: A
children.text: a class of securities required to be registered under section 12 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and

children.label: B
children.text: a class of securities which would be required to be so registered except for the exemption from registration provided in subsection (g)(2)(H) of such section 12.

children.label: A
children.text: the plan permits each participant 1 vote with respect to such issue, and

children.label: B
children.text: the trustee votes the shares held by the plan in the proportion determined after application of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Plan must be established before employer’s due date
children.label: f Plan must be established before employer’s due date

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A plan meets the requirements of this subsection only if it is established on or before the due date (including any extension of such date) for the filing of the employer’s tax return for the first taxable year of the employer for which an employee plan credit is claimed by the employer with respect to the plan.

children.heading: Special rule for first year
children.label: 2 Special rule for first year
children.text: A plan which otherwise meets the requirements of this section shall not be considered to have failed to meet the requirements of section 401(a) merely because it was not established by the close of the first taxable year of the employer for which an employee plan credit is claimed by the employer with respect to the plan.

children.heading: Transferred amounts must stay in plan even though investment credit is redetermined or recaptured
children.label: g Transferred amounts must stay in plan even though investment credit is redetermined or recaptured
children.text: A plan meets the requirement of this subsection only if it provides that amounts which are transferred to the plan (because of the requirements of section 48(n)(1) or 41(c)(1)(B)) shall remain in the plan (and, if allocated under the plan, shall remain so allocated) even though part or all of the employee plan credit or the credit allowed under section 41 (relating to employee stock ownership credit) is recaptured or redetermined. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the references to section 48(n)(1) and the employee plan credit shall refer to such section and credit as in effect before the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1984.111

children.heading: Right to demand employer securities; put option
children.label: h Right to demand employer securities; put option
A
children.text: has a right to demand that his benefits be distributed in the form of employer securities, and

children.label: B
children.text: if the employer securities are not readily tradable on an established market, has a right to require that the employer repurchase employer securities under a fair valuation formula.

children.heading: Plan may distribute cash in certain cases
children.label: 2 Plan may distribute cash in certain cases

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A plan which otherwise meets the requirements of this subsection or of section 4975(e)(7) shall not be considered to have failed to meet the requirements of section 401(a) merely because under the plan the benefits may be distributed in cash or in the form of employer securities.

children.heading: Exception for certain plans restricted from distributing securities
children.label: B Exception for certain plans restricted from distributing securities

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A plan to which this subparagraph applies shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of this subsection or section 401(a) merely because it does not permit a participant to exercise the right described in paragraph (1)(A) if such plan provides that the participant entitled to a distribution has a right to receive the distribution in cash, except that such plan may distribute employer securities subject to a requirement that such securities may be resold to the employer under terms which meet the requirements of paragraph (1)(B).

children.label: I
children.text: an employer whose charter or bylaws restrict the ownership of substantially all outstanding employer securities to employees or to a trust described in section 401(a), or

children.label: II
children.text: an S corporation.

children.heading: Special rule for banks
children.label: 3 Special rule for banks
children.text: In the case of a plan established and maintained by a bank (as defined in section 581) which is prohibited by law from redeeming or purchasing its own securities, the requirements of paragraph (1)(B) shall not apply if the plan provides that participants entitled to a distribution from the plan shall have a right to receive a distribution in cash.

children.heading: Put option period
children.label: 4 Put option period
children.text: An employer shall be deemed to satisfy the requirements of paragraph (1)(B) if it provides a put option for a period of at least 60 days following the date of distribution of stock of the employer and, if the put option is not exercised within such 60-day period, for an additional period of at least 60 days in the following plan year (as provided in regulations promulgated by the Secretary).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount to be paid for the employer securities is paid in substantially equal periodic payments (not less frequently than annually) over a period beginning not later than 30 days after the exercise of the put option described in paragraph (4) and not exceeding 5 years, and

children.label: B
children.text: there is adequate security provided and reasonable interest paid on the unpaid amounts referred to in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Payment requirement for installment distributions
children.label: 6 Payment requirement for installment distributions
children.text: If an employer is required to repurchase employer securities as part of an installment distribution, the requirements of paragraph (1)(B) shall be treated as met if the amount to be paid for the employer securities is paid not later than 30 days after the exercise of the put option described in paragraph (4).

children.heading: Exception where employee elected diversification
children.label: 7 Exception where employee elected diversification
children.text: Paragraph (1)(A) shall not apply with respect to the portion of the participant’s account which the employee elected to have reinvested under section 401(a)(28)(B) or subparagraph (B) or (C) of section 401(a)(35).

children.heading: Reimbursement for expenses of establishing and administering plan
children.label: i Reimbursement for expenses of establishing and administering plan
A
children.text: 10 percent of the first $100,000 which the employer is required to transfer to the plan for that taxable year under section 41(c)(1)(B), and1

children.label: B
children.text: 5 percent of any amount so required to be transferred in excess of the first $100,000; and

children.label: i
children.text: 10 percent of the first $100,000 of the dividends paid to the plan with respect to stock of the employer during the plan year ending with or within the employer’s taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: 5 percent of the amount of such dividends in excess of $100,000 or

children.heading: Conditional contributions to the plan
children.label: j Conditional contributions to the plan

children.text: the contribution to the plan is conditioned on a determination by the Secretary that such plan meets the requirements of this section,
the application for a determination described in paragraph (1) is filed with the Secretary not later than 90 days after the date on which an employee plan credit is claimed, and
the contribution is returned within 1 year after the date on which the Secretary issues notice to the employer that such plan does not satisfy the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Requirements relating to certain withdrawals
children.label: k Requirements relating to certain withdrawals

children.text: the withdrawal from a plan which otherwise meets the requirements of this section by the employer of an amount contributed for purposes of the matching employee plan credit shall not be considered to make the benefits forfeitable, and
the plan shall not, by reason of such withdrawal, fail to be for the exclusive benefit of participants or their beneficiaries,

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “employer securities” means common stock issued by the employer (or by a corporation which is a member of the same controlled group) which is readily tradable on an established securities market.

children.heading: Special rule where there is no readily tradable common stock
children.label: 2 Special rule where there is no readily tradable common stock
A
children.text: that class of common stock of the employer (or of any other such corporation) having the greatest voting power, and

children.label: B
children.text: that class of common stock of the employer (or of any other such corporation) having the greatest dividend rights.

children.heading: Preferred stock may be issued in certain cases
children.label: 3 Preferred stock may be issued in certain cases
children.text: Noncallable preferred stock shall be treated as employer securities if such stock is convertible at any time into stock which meets the requirements of paragraph (1) or (2) (whichever is applicable) and if such conversion is at a conversion price which (as of the date of the acquisition by the tax credit employee stock ownership plan) is reasonable. For purposes of the preceding sentence, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, preferred stock shall be treated as noncallable if after the call there will be a reasonable opportunity for a conversion which meets the requirements of the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Application to controlled group of corporations
children.label: 4 Application to controlled group of corporations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “controlled group of corporations” has the meaning given to such term by section 1563(a) (determined without regard to subsections (a)(4) and (e)(3)(C) of section 1563).

children.heading: Where common parent owns at least 50 percent of first tier subsidiary
children.label: B Where common parent owns at least 50 percent of first tier subsidiary
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), if the common parent owns directly stock possessing at least 50 percent of the voting power of all classes of stock and at least 50 percent of each class of nonvoting stock in a first tier subsidiary, such subsidiary (and all other corporations below it in the chain which would meet the 80 percent test of section 1563(a) if the first tier subsidiary were the common parent) shall be treated as includible corporations.

children.heading: Where common parent owns 100 percent of first tier subsidiary
children.label: C Where common parent owns 100 percent of first tier subsidiary
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), if the common parent owns directly stock possessing all of the voting power of all classes of stock and all of the nonvoting stock, in a first tier subsidiary, and if the first tier subsidiary owns directly stock possessing at least 50 percent of the voting power of all classes of stock, and at least 50 percent of each class of nonvoting stock, in a second tier subsidiary of the common parent, such second tier subsidiary (and all other corporations below it in the chain which would meet the 80 percent test of section 1563(a) if the second tier subsidiary were the common parent) shall be treated as includible corporations.

children.heading: Nonvoting common stock may be acquired in certain cases
children.label: 5 Nonvoting common stock may be acquired in certain cases
children.text: Nonvoting common stock of an employer described in the second sentence of section 401(a)(22) shall be treated as employer securities if an employer has a class of nonvoting common stock outstanding and the specific shares that the plan acquires have been issued and outstanding for at least 24 months.

children.heading: Nonrecognition of gain or loss on contribution of employer securities to tax credit employee stock ownership plan
children.label: m Nonrecognition of gain or loss on contribution of employer securities to tax credit employee stock ownership plan
children.text: No gain or loss shall be recognized to the taxpayer with respect to the transfer of employer securities to a tax credit employee stock ownership plan maintained by the taxpayer to the extent that such transfer is required under section 41(c)(1)(B), or subparagraph (A) or (B) of section 48(n)(1).11

children.heading: Securities received in certain transactions
children.label: n Securities received in certain transactions
i
children.text: any taxpayer who makes an election under section 1042(a) with respect to employer securities,

children.label: ii
children.text: any individual who is related to the taxpayer (within the meaning of section 267(b)), or

children.label: i
children.text: any class of outstanding stock of the corporation which issued such employer securities or of any corporation which is a member of the same controlled group of corporations (within the meaning of subsection ()(4)) as such corporation, orl

children.label: ii
children.text: the total value of any class of outstanding stock of any such corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: the plan shall be treated as having distributed to the person described in paragraph (1) the amount allocated to the account of such person in violation of paragraph (1) at the time of such allocation,

children.label: B
children.text: the provisions of section 4979A shall apply, and

children.label: i
children.text: the 1st allocation of employer securities in connection with a sale to the plan to which section 1042 applies, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the Secretary is notified of such failure.

children.label: i
children.text: such individual is a lineal descendant of the taxpayer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate amount allocated to the benefit of all such lineal descendants during the nonallocation period does not exceed more than 5 percent of the employer securities (or amounts allocated in lieu thereof) held by the plan which are attributable to a sale to the plan by any person related to such descendants (within the meaning of section 267(c)(4)) in a transaction to which section 1042 applied.

children.label: i
children.text: at any time during the 1-year period ending on the date of sale of qualified securities to the plan or cooperative, or

children.label: ii
children.text: on the date as of which qualified securities are allocated to participants in the plan or cooperative.

children.label: i
children.text: the date which is 10 years after the date of sale, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of the plan allocation attributable to the final payment of acquisition indebtedness incurred in connection with such sale.

children.label: i
children.text: in which the participant separates from service by reason of the attainment of normal retirement age under the plan, disability, or death, or

children.label: ii
children.text: which is the 5th plan year following the plan year in which the participant otherwise separates from service, except that this clause shall not apply if the participant is reemployed by the employer before distribution is required to begin under this clause.

children.heading: Exception for certain financed securities
children.label: B Exception for certain financed securities
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the account balance of a participant shall not include any employer securities acquired with the proceeds of the loan described in section 404(a)(9) until the close of the plan year in which such loan is repaid in full.

children.label: i
children.text: 5 years, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a participant with an account balance in excess of $800,000, 5 years plus 1 additional year (but not more than 5 additional years) for each $160,000 or fraction thereof by which such balance exceeds $800,000.

children.heading: Cost-of-living adjustment
children.label: 2 Cost-of-living adjustment
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust the dollar amounts under paragraph (1)(C) at the same time and in the same manner as under section 415(d).

children.heading: Prohibited allocations of securities in an S corporation
children.label: p Prohibited allocations of securities in an S corporation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An employee stock ownership plan holding employer securities consisting of stock in an S corporation shall provide that no portion of the assets of the plan attributable to (or allocable in lieu of) such employer securities may, during a nonallocation year, accrue (or be allocated directly or indirectly under any plan of the employer meeting the requirements of section 401(a)) for the benefit of any disqualified person.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a plan fails to meet the requirements of paragraph (1), the plan shall be treated as having distributed to any disqualified person the amount allocated to the account of such person in violation of paragraph (1) at the time of such allocation.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: B Cross reference
children.text: For excise tax relating to violations of paragraph (1) and ownership of synthetic equity, see section 4979A.

children.label: i
children.text: such plan holds employer securities consisting of stock in an S corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: disqualified persons own at least 50 percent of the number of shares of stock in the S corporation.

children.label: I
children.text: in applying paragraph (1) thereof, the members of an individual’s family shall include members of the family described in paragraph (4)(D), and

children.label: II
children.text: paragraph (4) thereof shall not apply.

children.heading: Deemed-owned shares
children.label: ii Deemed-owned shares
children.text: Notwithstanding the employee trust exception in section 318(a)(2)(B)(i), an individual shall be treated as owning deemed-owned shares of the individual.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate number of deemed-owned shares of such person and the members of such person’s family is at least 20 percent of the number of deemed-owned shares of stock in the S corporation, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a person not described in clause (i), the number of deemed-owned shares of such person is at least 10 percent of the number of deemed-owned shares of stock in such corporation.

children.heading: Treatment of family members
children.label: B Treatment of family members
children.text: In the case of a disqualified person described in subparagraph (A)(i), any member of such person’s family with deemed-owned shares shall be treated as a disqualified person if not otherwise treated as a disqualified person under subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: the stock in the S corporation constituting employer securities of an employee stock ownership plan which is allocated to such person under the plan, and

children.label: II
children.text: such person’s share of the stock in such corporation which is held by such plan but which is not allocated under the plan to participants.

children.heading: Person’s share of unallocated stock
children.label: ii Person’s share of unallocated stock
children.text: For purposes of clause (i)(II), a person’s share of unallocated S corporation stock held by such plan is the amount of the unallocated stock which would be allocated to such person if the unallocated stock were allocated to all participants in the same proportions as the most recent stock allocation under the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: the spouse of the individual,

children.label: ii
children.text: an ancestor or lineal descendant of the individual or the individual’s spouse,

children.label: iii
children.text: a brother or sister of the individual or the individual’s spouse and any lineal descendant of the brother or sister, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the spouse of any individual described in clause (ii) or (iii).

children.label: A
children.text: the treatment of any person as a disqualified person, or

children.label: B
children.text: the treatment of any year as a nonallocation year.

children.heading: Employee stock ownership plan
children.label: A Employee stock ownership plan
children.text: The term “employee stock ownership plan” has the meaning given such term by section 4975(e)(7).

children.heading: Employer securities
children.label: B Employer securities
children.text: The term “employer security” has the meaning given such term by section 409().l

children.heading: Synthetic equity
children.label: C Synthetic equity
children.text: The term “synthetic equity” means any stock option, warrant, restricted stock, deferred issuance stock right, or similar interest or right that gives the holder the right to acquire or receive stock of the S corporation in the future. Except to the extent provided in regulations, synthetic equity also includes a stock appreciation right, phantom stock unit, or similar right to a future cash payment based on the value of such stock or appreciation in such value.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Avoidance or evasion
children.label: B Avoidance or evasion
children.text: The Secretary may, by regulation or other guidance of general applicability, provide that a nonallocation year occurs in any case in which the principal purpose of the ownership structure of an S corporation constitutes an avoidance or evasion of this subsection.
